item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this one would be friends with SCP-1046

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Show spoiler
- Hide spoiler

Harrison Ford

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As SCP-1988 is spread by residence relocation, agents embedded in real estate firms in Chicago, IL; Dover, DE; and Portland, ME are to artificially alter the market such that it becomes an unfavorable decision to relocate out of the city

1) So they'd lower the prices of pre-1971 houses so much that, at the price the homeowners could get for them, they couldn't afford to move anywhere else?

2) Just a few embedded agents wouldn't be able to do that: their bosses would notice that they were doing funny things with the prices. The Foundation would have to control all the real-estate agencies in the affected cities.

3) Someone would notice something weird was happening to the real-estate market in the area.

Heat-transfer appliances, such as refrigerators and ovens, will begin to reverse or otherwise change the direction in which they transfer heat.

Ovens don't work by transferring heat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alrighty-dighty, revisions have been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, dude! I don't usually recognize the non-major towns mentioned in SCPs.
The only famous person I know from Park Ridge is Hillary Clinton, though, so I just had to ignore the black boxes…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, fair enough. Revision will commence in the morning when I'm not bone-tired and so sick I can't breathe without everything smelling like ground beef and armpit con frijoles. Thanks for the feedback. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with everything written here, with the addition that the house be knocked down and it written off as it having been marked for asanation, and the inhabitants given replacement housing as is often the case in such cases.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1988 is a communicable infection of bioarchitectural nature that affects houses built before 1961

When an infected house is discovered, the occupants are to be transferred to temporary residence that lacks insulation, electricity, and plumbing

All features infected with SCP-1988 are to be burnt or smelted down and replaced with replicas; in the later stages of infection, this will most likely necessitate completely rebuilding the house.

The occupants are then to be given Class-A Amnestics and reintroduced to their house with emphasis that they are moving into it for the first time.

This seems like it would create more problems then it would solve. How much is it costing to house the occupants in a third world shack during the remodel. I assume they would basically be prisoners at this point. How do you explain their absence from work or school while the remodel is being done? How do you deal with the neighbors when they move back? If the original occupants believe they are moving in for the first time, how are the neighbors going to react. How do you explain a remodel extensive enough to rebuild an entire house if you replace everything with replicas so it looks exactly the same as before?

Wouldn't it just be easier to have them find a great deal on a new place built after 1961, give them some vouchers to buy new energy efficient appliances as part of some government program, and redevelop the infected property?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, that's awesome. Both the inspiration for the skip and the (spoilery?) fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Yep, he was my dad's buddy in high school. This skip was inspired by a story my dad told me when Harrison had bronchitis as a kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Waidaminute.
That name.
Is it

+ show block
– hide block

Harrison Ford?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of Dionaea House. Which is fine because I really like Dionaea House.

I like the infection aspect of it, although I'm not really feeling the "awareness = increased speed" thing. But yeah, overall I liked it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, you're right. The Keter thing was before I had finalized the draft and I didn't re-evaluate it. Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone used one of my articles as an example. Made mah day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehhhhh. I don't like the element of compulsion, and the house having organs and such reminds me a lot of SCP-1046. Also the house only progressing in its "symptoms" if people are aware of it seems kind of tacked-on.

Seconding the classification issues. As a contagious phenomenon, this is a Euclid. Keter? I'm assuming that's related to the "greenhouse gases" thing, which seems ridiculous; just how much carbon dioxide is being released into the atmosphere from anomalous breathing houses? How many of these things are there? You're telling me they're more of a problem than a bunch of coal-fired power plants?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if Keter.

The greenhouse effect line feels tacked on for added danger. That's the kind of conclusion a reader can come to on their own. That said, I got to the end and went, "That's it?" I kind of feel like this needs something else, though I'm not sure what. It's definitely a great idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, just an actor…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would you want to be inside a house that was suffocating to death?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woo, Chicago suburbs represent!
In all seriousness, I think this is a pretty good "house what is alive" skip, so it has got my upvote.
Don't 100% agree with it being keter, but it's close enough to not be a major issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds like a virus that somehow makes houses feel like they're haunted by a ghost. An interesting take on that phenomenon, but the lack of apparent purpose or malice behind it (besides contributing to elevated carbon dioxide) makes the entry fizzle out at the end, and lose its punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So who's behind the black boxes, patient zero?
It's possible I'm being dense and missing hints, but I've got not a clue; and I feel like it would be an interesting twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be a good way to cut down the instances and make containment easier, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting.

I wonder what happens if you try to poison the house by spraying toxins from inside? Does the house die?

And what about moving in mentally handicapped people during stage two?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I said I'd upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
deep breath in

long breath out
